slices.name: SecurityKernel
slices.code:
84 | class SecurityKernel:
  85 |     SENSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  86 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----.*?-----END[A-Z0-9 ]+KEY-----", re.DOTALL),
  87 |         re.compile(r"-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----.*?-----END CERTIFICATE-----", re.DOTALL),
  88 |         re.compile(r"(api_key|secret_key|auth_token)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}['\"]", re.IGNORECASE),
  89 |     ]
  90 | 
  91 |     @staticmethod
  92 |     def is_binary(filepath: str, scan_bytes: int = 2048) -> bool:
  93 |         if any(filepath.lower().endswith(ext) for ext in IGNORE_EXTENSIONS):
  94 |             return True
  95 |         try:
  96 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as handle:
  97 |                 chunk = handle.read(scan_bytes)
  98 |             if not chunk:
  99 |                 return False
 100 |             if b'\x00' in chunk:
 101 |                 return True
 102 |             guess, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(filepath)
 103 |             if guess and not guess.startswith(('text', 'application')):
 104 |                 return True
 105 |             # Fallback heuristic: high non-text ratio
 106 |             text_chars = bytearray({7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 27} | set(range(0x20, 0x7F)))
 107 |             nontext = sum(byte not in text_chars for byte in chunk)
 108 |             return (nontext / len(chunk)) > 0.40
 109 |         except Exception:
 110 |             return True
 111 | 
 112 |     @staticmethod
 113 |     def calculate_entropy(text: str) -> float:
 114 |         if not text:
 115 |             return 0.0
 116 |         entropy = 0.0
 117 |         for idx in range(256):
 118 |             p_x = float(text.count(chr(idx))) / len(text)
 119 |             if p_x > 0:
 120 |                 entropy -= p_x * math.log(p_x, 2)
 121 |         return entropy
 122 | 
 123 |     @classmethod
 124 |     def sanitize_content(cls, content: str) -> str:
 125 |         # 1) Block-level regex redaction
 126 |         for pattern in cls.SENSITIVE_PATTERNS:
 127 |             content = pattern.sub("[REDACTED_SENSITIVE_PATTERN]", content)
 128 | 
 129 |         # 2) Line-level entropy redaction
 130 |         sanitized_lines: List[str] = []
 131 |         for line in content.splitlines():
 132 |             if any(key in line.lower() for key in ['api', 'key', 'secret', 'token', 'auth', 'password']):
 133 |                 if cls.calculate_entropy(line) > 4.5:
 134 |                     parts = line.split('=', 1)
 135 |                     prefix = parts[0] if len(parts) == 2 else line.split(':', 1)[0]
 136 |                     sanitized_lines.append(f"{prefix}= [REDACTED_HIGH_ENTROPY]")
 137 |                     continue
 138 |             sanitized_lines.append(line)
 139 |         return "\n".join(sanitized_lines)

  94 |             return True
  95 |         try:
  96 |             with open(filepath, 'rb') as handle:
  97 |                 chunk = handle.read(scan_bytes)
  98 |             if not chunk:
  99 |                 return False
 100 |             if b'\x00' in chunk:
 101 |                 return True

 103 |             if guess and not guess.startswith(('text', 'application')):
 104 |                 return True
 105 |             # Fallback heuristic: high non-text ratio
 106 |             text_chars = bytearray({7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 27} | set(range(0x20, 0x7F)))
 107 |             nontext = sum(byte not in text_chars for byte in chunk)
 108 |             return (nontext / len(chunk)) > 0.40
 109 |         except Exception:
 110 |             return True

 118 |             p_x = float(text.count(chr(idx))) / len(text)

slices.name: PolyglotAnalyzer
slices.code:
145 | class PolyglotAnalyzer:
 146 |     def __init__(self, event_callback=None) -> None:
 147 |         self.callback = event_callback
 148 | 
 149 |     def analyze_python(self, content: str, filename: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 150 |         stats: PyStats = {"functions": [], "classes": [], "imports": 0}
 151 |         try:
 152 |             tree = ast.parse(content)
 153 |             for node in ast.walk(tree):
 154 |                 if isinstance(node, (ast.Import, ast.ImportFrom)):
 155 |                     stats["imports"] += 1
 156 |                     if self.callback:
 157 |                         module_name = node.module if isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom) else node.names[0].name
 158 |                         self.callback("dependency_detected", {"source_file": filename, "module": module_name})
 159 |                 elif isinstance(node, ast.FunctionDef):
 160 |                     stats["functions"].append(node.name)
 161 |                 elif isinstance(node, ast.ClassDef):
 162 |                     stats["classes"].append(node.name)
 163 |             return {"summary": stats, "complexity": stats["imports"]}
 164 |         except SyntaxError:
 165 |             return {"summary": {"error": "Syntax Error"}, "complexity": 999}
 166 |         except Exception:
 167 |             return {"summary": {}, "complexity": 0}
 168 | 
 169 |     def analyze_json(self, content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 170 |         try:
 171 |             data = json.loads(content)
 172 |             keys = list(data.keys()) if isinstance(data, dict) else ["<array>"]
 173 |             return {"summary": {"keys": keys[:10]}, "complexity": 0}
 174 |         except json.JSONDecodeError:
 175 |             return {"summary": {"error": "Invalid JSON"}, "complexity": 0}
 176 | 
 177 |     def analyze_markdown(self, content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 178 |         headers = re.findall(r'^#{1,3}\s+(.*)', content, re.MULTILINE)
 179 |         return {"summary": {"headers": headers[:10]}, "complexity": 0}
 180 | 
 181 |     def analyze_yaml(self, content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 182 |         """Heuristic YAML top-level key extractor without external deps."""
 183 |         keys: List[str] = []
 184 |         try:
 185 |             for line in content.splitlines():
 186 |                 match = re.match(r'^([a-zA-Z0-9_-]+):', line)
 187 |                 if match:
 188 |                     keys.append(match.group(1))
 189 |             return {"summary": {"keys": keys[:15]}, "complexity": 0}
 190 |         except Exception:
 191 |             return {"summary": {}, "complexity": 0}
 192 | 
 193 |     def analyze(self, filename: str, content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
 194 |         ext = pathlib.Path(filename).suffix.lower()
 195 |         if ext == '.py':
 196 |             return self.analyze_python(content, filename)
 197 |         if ext == '.json':
 198 |             return self.analyze_json(content)
 199 |         if ext in ['.yaml', '.yml']:
 200 |             return self.analyze_yaml(content)
 201 |         if ext in ['.md', '.markdown']:
 202 |             return self.analyze_markdown(content)
 203 |         return {"summary": {}, "complexity": 0}

slices.name: traverse_project
slices.code:
209 | def traverse_project(root_path: pathlib.Path):
 210 |     stack: List[pathlib.Path] = [root_path]
 211 |     while stack:
 212 |         current = stack.pop()
 213 |         if not current.is_dir():
 214 |             continue
 215 |         try:
 216 |             with os.scandir(current) as entries:
 217 |                 for entry in sorted(entries, key=lambda e: e.name):
 218 |                     if entry.name in IGNORE_DIRS or entry.name.startswith('.'):
 219 |                         continue
 220 |                     if entry.is_dir(follow_symlinks=False):
 221 |                         stack.append(pathlib.Path(entry.path))
 222 |                     elif entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=False):
 223 |                         yield pathlib.Path(entry.path)
 224 |         except PermissionError:
 225 |             continue

slices.name: WorkspacePackager
slices.code:
231 | class WorkspacePackager:
 232 |     def __init__(self, root_path: pathlib.Path, event_callback=None):
 233 |         self.root = root_path.resolve()
 234 |         self.event_callback = event_callback
 235 |         self.files_registry: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
 236 |         self.scan_stats: ScanStats = {
 237 |             "bundled": 0,
 238 |             "skipped": 0,
 239 |             "errors": 0,
 240 |             "skipped_details": {
 241 |                 "binary_or_ext": 0,
 242 |                 "oversize": 0,
 243 |             },
 244 |             "skipped_by_ext": {},
 245 |         }
 246 |         self.timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
 247 | 
 248 |     def _track_skip(self, reason: Literal["binary_or_ext", "oversize"], ext: str) -> None:
 249 |         self.scan_stats['skipped'] += 1
 250 |         if reason == "binary_or_ext":
 251 |             self.scan_stats['skipped_details']["binary_or_ext"] += 1
 252 |         else:
 253 |             self.scan_stats['skipped_details']["oversize"] += 1
 254 |         safe_ext = (ext or "").lower()
 255 |         self.scan_stats['skipped_by_ext'][safe_ext] = self.scan_stats['skipped_by_ext'].get(safe_ext, 0) + 1
 256 | 
 257 |     def _generate_tree_map(self) -> str:
 258 |         lines: List[str] = [f"{self.root.name}/"]
 259 |         paths = sorted(pathlib.Path(f['path']) for f in self.files_registry)
 260 |         seen_dirs: set[str] = set()
 261 |         for p in paths:
 262 |             parts = p.parts
 263 |             indent = 0
 264 |             for i, part in enumerate(parts[:-1]):
 265 |                 d = pathlib.Path(*parts[:i + 1])
 266 |                 if str(d) not in seen_dirs:
 267 |                     lines.append(f"{'  ' * (indent + 1)}|-- {part}/")
 268 |                     seen_dirs.add(str(d))
 269 |                 indent += 1
 270 |             lines.append(f"{'  ' * (indent + 1)}|-- {parts[-1]}")
 271 |         return "\n".join(lines)
 272 | 
 273 |     def _generate_text_output(self) -> str:
 274 |         lines: List[str] = [
 275 |             f"# Workspace Bundle: {self.root.name}",
 276 |             f"# Generated: {self.timestamp}",
 277 |             "# Compliance: os.scandir traversal; binary guard; PEM + entropy redaction; polyglot summaries",
 278 |             "",
 279 |             "# Project Structure:",
 280 |         ]
 281 |         lines.extend(f"# {ln}" for ln in self._generate_tree_map().splitlines())
 282 |         lines.append("")
 283 | 
 284 |         for f in self.files_registry:
 285 |             lines.append(f"--- FILE: {f['path']} ---")
 286 |             lines.append(f"Size: {f['size_bytes']} bytes")
 287 |             summary = f['summary']
 288 |             if summary:
 289 |                 parts = []
 290 |                 if summary.get("classes"):
 291 |                     parts.append("Classes: " + ", ".join(summary["classes"]))
 292 |                 if summary.get("functions"):
 293 |                     parts.append("Functions: " + ", ".join(summary["functions"]))
 294 |                 if summary.get("keys"):
 295 |                     parts.append("Keys: " + ", ".join(summary["keys"]))
 296 |                 if summary.get("headers"):
 297 |                     parts.append("Headers: " + ", ".join(summary["headers"]))
 298 |                 lines.append(f"Summary: {'; '.join(parts)}")
 299 |             else:
 300 |                 lines.append("Summary: (none)")
 301 |             lines.append("Content: |")
 302 |             for line in SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(f['content']).splitlines():
 303 |                 lines.append(f"  {line}")
 304 |             lines.append("")
 305 |         return "\n".join(lines)
 306 | 
 307 |     def _generate_json_output(self) -> str:
 308 |         payload = {
 309 |             "meta": {
 310 |                 "project": self.root.name,
 311 |                 "generated_at": self.timestamp,
 312 |                 "stats": self.scan_stats,
 313 |                 "tree": self._generate_tree_map(),
 314 |             },
 315 |             "files": [
 316 |                 {
 317 |                     "path": f['path'],
 318 |                     "size_bytes": f['size_bytes'],
 319 |                     "complexity": f['complexity'],
 320 |                     "summary": f['summary'],
 321 |                     "content": SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(f['content']),
 322 |                 }
 323 |                 for f in self.files_registry
 324 |             ],
 325 |         }
 326 |         return json.dumps(payload, indent=2)
 327 | 
 328 |     def _generate_xml_output(self) -> str:
 329 |         lines: List[str] = ['<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>', '<workspace>']
 330 |         lines.append(f'  <meta project="{self.root.name}" generated="{self.timestamp}">')
 331 |         lines.append("    <tree><![CDATA[")
 332 |         lines.append(self._generate_tree_map())
 333 |         lines.append("    ]]></tree>")
 334 |         lines.append("  </meta>")
 335 |         lines.append("  <files>")
 336 |         for f in self.files_registry:
 337 |             lines.append(f'    <file path="{f["path"]}" size="{f["size_bytes"]}" complexity="{f["complexity"]}">')
 338 |             if f['summary']:
 339 |                 s_text = str(f['summary']).replace("&", "&").replace("<", "<").replace(">", ">")
 340 |                 lines.append(f"      <summary>{s_text}</summary>")
 341 |             lines.append("      <content><![CDATA[")
 342 |             lines.append(SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(f['content']))
 343 |             lines.append("      ]]></content>")
 344 |             lines.append("    </file>")
 345 |         lines.append("  </files>")
 346 |         lines.append("</workspace>")
 347 |         return "\n".join(lines)
 348 | 
 349 |     def run(self, format_type: str = "text") -> str:
 350 |         print(f"Scanning root: {self.root}")
 351 |         analyzer = PolyglotAnalyzer(event_callback=self.event_callback)
 352 |         for path_obj in traverse_project(self.root):
 353 |             try:
 354 |                 if SecurityKernel.is_binary(str(path_obj)):
 355 |                     self._track_skip("binary_or_ext", path_obj.suffix)
 356 |                     continue
 357 |                 size = path_obj.stat().st_size
 358 |                 if size > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES:
 359 |                     self._track_skip("oversize", path_obj.suffix)
 360 |                     continue
 361 |                 try:
 362 |                     with open(path_obj, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
 363 |                         raw = handle.read()
 364 |                 except Exception:
 365 |                     self.scan_stats['errors'] += 1
 366 |                     continue
 367 | 
 368 |                 analysis = analyzer.analyze(path_obj.name, raw)
 369 | 
 370 |                 self.files_registry.append({
 371 |                     "path": path_obj.relative_to(self.root).as_posix(),
 372 |                     "size_bytes": size,
 373 |                     "content": raw,
 374 |                     "summary": analysis.get("summary"),
 375 |                     "complexity": analysis.get("complexity", 0),
 376 |                 })
 377 |                 self.scan_stats['bundled'] += 1
 378 |             except Exception:
 379 |                 self.scan_stats['errors'] += 1
 380 | 
 381 |         self.files_registry.sort(key=lambda x: (x['complexity'], x['path']))
 382 |         print("Completed.")
 383 |         print(f"  Bundled: {self.scan_stats['bundled']}")
 384 |         print(f"  Skipped: {self.scan_stats['skipped']}")
 385 |         print(f"    - Binary/Ignored Ext: {self.scan_stats['skipped_details']['binary_or_ext']}")
 386 |         print(f"    - Oversize (>1.5MB):  {self.scan_stats['skipped_details']['oversize']}")
 387 |         if self.scan_stats["skipped_by_ext"]:
 388 |             print("  Skipped by extension:")
 389 |             for ext, count in sorted(self.scan_stats["skipped_by_ext"].items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1], reverse=True):
 390 |                 label = ext or "<none>"
 391 |                 print(f"    {label}: {count}")
 392 |         print(f"  Errors:  {self.scan_stats['errors']}")
 393 | 
 394 |         if format_type == "json":
 395 |             return self._generate_json_output()
 396 |         if format_type == "xml":
 397 |             return self._generate_xml_output()
 398 |         return self._generate_text_output()

 316 |                 {
 317 |                     "path": f['path'],
 318 |                     "size_bytes": f['size_bytes'],
 319 |                     "complexity": f['complexity'],
 320 |                     "summary": f['summary'],
 321 |                     "content": SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(f['content']),
 322 |                 }
 323 |                 for f in self.files_registry
 324 |             ],
 325 |         }
 326 |         return json.dumps(payload, indent=2)

slices.name: _generate_xml_output
slices.code:
328 |     def _generate_xml_output(self) -> str:
 329 |         lines: List[str] = ['<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>', '<workspace>']
 330 |         lines.append(f'  <meta project="{self.root.name}" generated="{self.timestamp}">')
 331 |         lines.append("    <tree><![CDATA[")
 332 |         lines.append(self._generate_tree_map())
 333 |         lines.append("    ]]></tree>")
 334 |         lines.append("  </meta>")
 335 |         lines.append("  <files>")
 336 |         for f in self.files_registry:
 337 |             lines.append(f'    <file path="{f["path"]}" size="{f["size_bytes"]}" complexity="{f["complexity"]}">')
 338 |             if f['summary']:
 339 |                 s_text = str(f['summary']).replace("&", "&").replace("<", "<").replace(">", ">")
 340 |                 lines.append(f"      <summary>{s_text}</summary>")
 341 |             lines.append("      <content><![CDATA[")
 342 |             lines.append(SecurityKernel.sanitize_content(f['content']))
 343 |             lines.append("      ]]></content>")
 344 |             lines.append("    </file>")
 345 |         lines.append("  </files>")
 346 |         lines.append("</workspace>")
 347 |         return "\n".join(lines)

slices.name: run
slices.code:
349 |     def run(self, format_type: str = "text") -> str:
 350 |         print(f"Scanning root: {self.root}")
 351 |         analyzer = PolyglotAnalyzer(event_callback=self.event_callback)
 352 |         for path_obj in traverse_project(self.root):
 353 |             try:
 354 |                 if SecurityKernel.is_binary(str(path_obj)):
 355 |                     self._track_skip("binary_or_ext", path_obj.suffix)
 356 |                     continue
 357 |                 size = path_obj.stat().st_size
 358 |                 if size > MAX_FILE_SIZE_BYTES:
 359 |                     self._track_skip("oversize", path_obj.suffix)
 360 |                     continue
 361 |                 try:
 362 |                     with open(path_obj, "r", encoding="utf-8", errors="ignore") as handle:
 363 |                         raw = handle.read()
 364 |                 except Exception:
 365 |                     self.scan_stats['errors'] += 1
 366 |                     continue
 367 | 
 368 |                 analysis = analyzer.analyze(path_obj.name, raw)
 369 | 
 370 |                 self.files_registry.append({
 371 |                     "path": path_obj.relative_to(self.root).as_posix(),
 372 |                     "size_bytes": size,
 373 |                     "content": raw,
 374 |                     "summary": analysis.get("summary"),
 375 |                     "complexity": analysis.get("complexity", 0),
 376 |                 })
 377 |                 self.scan_stats['bundled'] += 1
 378 |             except Exception:
 379 |                 self.scan_stats['errors'] += 1
 380 | 
 381 |         self.files_registry.sort(key=lambda x: (x['complexity'], x['path']))
 382 |         print("Completed.")
 383 |         print(f"  Bundled: {self.scan_stats['bundled']}")
 384 |         print(f"  Skipped: {self.scan_stats['skipped']}")
 385 |         print(f"    - Binary/Ignored Ext: {self.scan_stats['skipped_details']['binary_or_ext']}")
 386 |         print(f"    - Oversize (>1.5MB):  {self.scan_stats['skipped_details']['oversize']}")
 387 |         if self.scan_stats["skipped_by_ext"]:
 388 |             print("  Skipped by extension:")
 389 |             for ext, count in sorted(self.scan_stats["skipped_by_ext"].items(), key=lambda kv: kv[1], reverse=True):
 390 |                 label = ext or "<none>"
 391 |                 print(f"    {label}: {count}")
 392 |         print(f"  Errors:  {self.scan_stats['errors']}")
 393 | 
 394 |         if format_type == "json":
 395 |             return self._generate_json_output()
 396 |         if format_type == "xml":
 397 |             return self._generate_xml_output()
 398 |         return self._generate_text_output()

slices.name: main
slices.code:
401 | def main() -> None:
 402 |     parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Aletheia Workspace Packager v2.3.1 (CLI)")
 403 |     parser.add_argument("path", nargs="?", default=".", help="Project root to package")
 404 |     parser.add_argument("--format", choices=["text", "json", "xml"], default="text", help="Output format")
 405 |     parser.add_argument("-o", "--output", help="Output filename")
 406 |     args = parser.parse_args()
 407 | 
 408 |     root_path = pathlib.Path(args.path).resolve()
 409 |     if not root_path.exists():
 410 |         sys.exit(f"Error: path does not exist: {root_path}")
 411 | 
 412 |     packager = WorkspacePackager(root_path)
 413 |     bundle_content = packager.run(args.format)
 414 | 
 415 |     if args.output:
 416 |         out_path = pathlib.Path(args.output)
 417 |     else:
 418 |         ts = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
 419 |         ext = {"text": "yaml", "json": "json", "xml": "xml"}[args.format]
 420 |         out_dir = pathlib.Path(f"{root_path.name}_bundle_{ts}")
 421 |         out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 422 |         out_path = out_dir / f"{root_path.name}_bundle_{ts}.{ext}"
 423 | 
 424 |     try:
 425 |         out_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 426 |         with open(out_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
 427 |             handle.write(bundle_content)
 428 |     except Exception as exc:
 429 |         sys.exit(f"Error writing output: {exc}")
 430 | 
 431 |     print(f"Bundle saved to: {out_path.resolve()}")
 432 |     print(f"Stats: {packager.scan_stats}")
